reasoning: The PKGBUILD is a standard VCS icon theme package. It clones from the official numixproject GitHub repo via git+https, uses SKIP checksums (normal for VCS), has a trivial pkgver() running git describe on the local clone, and a package() that merely copies icon directories to /usr/share/icons. There is no prepare/build with code execution, no .install hook, no network fetches beyond the declared source, and no obfuscation. deepseek-v4-flash additionally reviewed all the git hook samples and the index.theme metadata file, all confirmed benign. The qwen review was shallow (PKGBUILD only) but the PKGBUILD is the only file that executes during makepkg, so the verdict still holds.
disagreement_analysis: There is no verdict disagreement — both models concluded 'safe'. The trigger was coverage depth: qwen reviewed only 1 file (PKGBUILD) while deepseek reviewed 11. The extra files deepseek examined (git hook .sample templates and an index.theme) are not executed by any PKGBUILD function or install hook, so qwen's shallow review did not miss anything security-relevant.
coverage_issues: qwen reviewed only the PKGBUILD and declined to review further files; while acceptable here since no other file is executed during makepkg, it provides less corroboration of package contents.
learnings: The 'shallow' trigger (1 file reviewed) is a weak signal for packages with no .install hook and no prepare/build logic — for pure icon/font/theme -git packages, PKGBUILD review alone is often sufficient since package() only copies static assets.

Git hook .sample files (hooks/*.sample) are standard Git templates that ship in cloned repos and are never executed by makepkg; the tool could deprioritize or auto-classify these to save tokens (deepseek spent effort reviewing 9 of them with predictable 'safe' results).

File selection should prioritize .install scripts and any files referenced inside build()/package()/prepare()/check(); here there were none, so the shallow review was effectively complete. The tool could detect 'no install hook + package() only does cp/install of theme dirs' and lower the shallow-review concern automatically.
Both models correctly identified SKIP checksums and git+https upstream as normal for VCS packages — reasoning quality was accurate and well-calibrated, no overreaction observed.
